title: fix: analyze and improve codebase — bugs, security, resource leaks, code quality
body:
## Summary

Full codebase analysis followed by targeted fixes across four priority tiers. All changes maintain Python 2/3 compatibility and do not break existing skin/config API.

---

## Commit 1 — Critical Bug Fixes

| Fix | File | Impact |
|-----|------|--------|
| `os_path` / `os_remove` → `os.path` / `os.remove` | `tools/logging.py:26-32` | `dellog()` ve `DreamOS()` hiçbir zaman çalışmıyordu |
| `connected_to_internet()` except bloğu her durumda "available" yazdırıyor | `RaedQuickWeather.py:70-72` | Her ağ hatası başarılı görünüyordu |
| `downloadFile()` fallback kodu erişilemez durumda (`return True` önce) | `plugin.py:212-224` | HTTP 403 için User-Agent fallback hiç çalışmamış |
| `'utf'` → `'utf-8'` codec adı `removeunicode()` içinde | `plugin.py:93` | Python 3'te `LookupError` sessizce yutuluyordu |

## Commit 2 — Security Fixes

| Fix | File | Impact |
|-----|------|--------|
| Global SSL doğrulama bypass'ı kaldırıldı | `tools/compat.py:7-10` | Tüm Enigma2 HTTPS bağlantılarını etkileyen MITM açığı |
| `verify=False` kaldırıldı (geonames isteği) | `plugin.py:260` | Doğrulanmamış TLS üzerinden şehir verisi çekiliyordu |
| `os.system('cp ...')` → `shutil.copy2()`, `os.system('rm ...')` → `os.remove()` | `__init__.py:19-22` | Shell subprocess ve injection riski giderildi |
| `weather_city` URL-encode edildi (shell komutuna enjeksiyon) | `RaedQuickWeather.py:420` | Kötü amaçlı şehir adıyla shell injection mümkündü |

## Commit 3 — Resource Leak Fixes (30+ unclosed file handles)

Tüm bare `open()` çağrıları `with` bloğuyla sarıldı. Gömülü cihazda her 3 saniyede bir poll yapılırken sızdırılan FD'ler birikip `OSError: [Errno 24] Too many open files` hatasına yol açıyordu.

**Etkilenen dosyalar:** `tools/logging.py`, `__init__.py`, `plugin.py`, `RaedQuickWeather.py`, `RaedQuickServiceInfo2.py`, `RaedQuickServName2.py`, `RaedQuickEcmInfo.py`, `RaedQuickSignalPicEmuF.py`

Ayrıca `write_none()` içindeki `with` bloğu sonrasındaki gereksiz `noneweather.close()` kaldırıldı.

## Commit 4 — Code Quality

| Fix | Dosya |
|-----|-------|
| `cElementTree` deprecated import → `try/except ImportError` guard (Python 3.9+ uyumu) | `plugin.py:37` |
| Modül-level `info={}` / `old_ecm_mtime` → instance variable `self._ecm_info` / `self._old_ecm_mtime` | `RaedQuickSignalCaidInfo2.py` |
| Bare `except:` → `except ImportError:` / `except (IOError, OSError):` / `except Exception:` | `plugin.py`, `__init__.py`, `logging.py` |
| `ecmfile()` içindeki kapatılmamış dosya handle'ı düzeltildi | `RaedQuickSignalCaidInfo2.py` |

## Commit 5 — URL Update

`https://github.com/fairbird/RaedQuickSignal` → `https://github.com/0x733/RaedQuickSignal` (5 dosya, 8 URL)

---

## Test plan

- [ ] `python3 -m py_compile` ile tüm değiştirilen dosyaların sözdizimi kontrol edildi ✅
- [ ] `dellog()` ve `DreamOS()` çağrıları artık `NameError` vermeden çalışır
- [ ] Ağ kapalıyken `connected_to_internet()` → `False` döner (artık `True` değil)
- [ ] `downloadFile()` ile `compat_urlretrieve` başarısız olduğunda fallback `compat_urlopen` çalışır
- [ ] `removeunicode(u"café")` Python 3'te `LookupError` atmaz
- [ ] 30 saniyelik poll döngüsünde FD sayısı sabit kalır (`lsof -p $(pidof enigma2) | wc -l`)
- [ ] Şehir config'ine `'; echo INJECTED > /tmp/proof;'` yazılıp weather refresh tetiklendiğinde `/tmp/proof` oluşmaz

title: Bump python-dotenv from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2
body:
Bumps [python-dotenv](https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2.
Release notes

Sourced from python-dotenv's releases.

v1.2.2

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by @​bbc2 in #790c5
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by @​JYOuyang in theskumar/python-dotenv#590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Misc

  • skip 000 permission tests for root user by @​burnout-projects in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • Bump actions/checkout from 5 to 6 in the github-actions group by @​dependabot[bot] in theskumar/python-dotenv#593

title: Adjust dependency pins for Python 3.14 compatibility (add version markers and pillow)
body:
### Motivation

- The app was failing to boot in newer Python runtimes because some pinned scientific packages and transitive `pillow` were being built from source and failing due to missing system libraries (e.g., `zlib`).
- The goal is to avoid source builds on Python 3.13+ by selecting wheel-friendly package ranges while preserving existing Python 3.11 pins.

### Description

- Updated `requirements.txt` to add Python-version-aware constraints for `numpy`, `pandas`, and `matplotlib` so older pins remain for Python 3.11 and relaxed wheel-friendly ranges apply for Python >= 3.13.
- Added an explicit `pillow>=11,<12` requirement so modern runtimes pick prebuilt wheels and avoid `zlib` compile failures when building from source.
- Added a short note to `README.md` explaining why the version markers were introduced and how they help on Python 3.14+ deployments.

### Testing

- Ran `python -m compileall app.py` and it completed successfully.
- Attempted `python -m pip install -r requirements.txt` but full dependency installation could not be validated because package index access is blocked in this environment (proxy returned `403 Forbidden`).

title: Downgrade python-telegram-bot to 20.5 for Python 3.13 compatibility
body:
## Problem

python-telegram-bot 20.6 (and 20.7) introduced a `__slots__` implementation in the `Updater` class that omits `__polling_cleanup_cb`, causing an `AttributeError` at runtime under Python 3.13.

## Solution

Downgraded `python-telegram-bot` from `20.6` to `20.5` in `requirements.txt`. Version 20.5 predates the broken `__slots__` change and is fully compatible with Python 3.13.

### Changes
- **Modified** `requirements.txt`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

title: Fix pydantic compatibility with Python 3.13
body:
## Problem

The build fails on Python 3.13 because pydantic 2.7.1 depends on pydantic-core 2.18.2, which only supports up to Python 3.12. The Rust compiler rejects the build with: `error: the configured Python interpreter version (3.13) is newer than PyO3's maximum supported version (3.12)`.

## Solution

Updated `pydantic` from 2.7.1 to 2.9.2 and `pydantic-settings` from 2.2.1 to 2.5.2 in `finance-backend/requirements.txt`. pydantic 2.9.x ships with pydantic-core 2.23.x, which added full Python 3.13 support. Both updates are backwards-compatible with the existing pydantic v2 API usage in the codebase.

### Changes
- **Modified** `finance-backend/requirements.txt`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

title: Fix SQLAlchemy compatibility with Python 3.13
body:
## Problem

SQLAlchemy 2.0.30 is incompatible with Python 3.13, causing a runtime crash on import with `TypeError: Can't replace canonical symbol for '__firstlineno__' with new int value 615`. This prevents the FastAPI backend from starting at all.

## Solution

Bumped `sqlalchemy` from `2.0.30` to `2.0.36` in `finance-backend/requirements.txt`. Version 2.0.36 includes the fix for Python 3.13's changes to symbol handling in the `__firstlineno__` attribute, restoring compatibility without requiring a major version jump.

### Changes
- **Modified** `finance-backend/requirements.txt`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

title: feat: incompatible attribute, uninstaller, PySide2 + Fluent Widgets, Python 3.9
body: ## Summary Eight changes in this PR: 1. **Component incompatibility logic** — Adds an `incompatible` attribute to the component schema (defaults to `[]` for existing items). When a user checks a component in the installer UI: - Incompatible components that are **unchecked** get disabled (greyed out) - If an incompatible component is **already checked**, a popup asks the user to choose which one to keep; the other is unchecked and disabled - When a component is **unchecked**, previously disabled incompatible components are re-enabled (unless still blocked by another active conflict or marked `disabled` in metadata) 2. **Uninstaller** (`uninstaller.py`) — New standalone PySide2 GUI application. During installation, the installer now tracks all extracted file paths per component and saves an `install_manifest.json` to the install directory, then copies `Uninstall.exe` alongside it. The uninstaller reads this manifest, presents a component selection tree, and removes selected files/directories. If the core component (defined by `main_item` in `metadata.json`) is removed, Windows registry entries are also cleaned up. After partial uninstall the manifest is updated to reflect remaining components. 3. **Password page removed** — `PasswordPage` class, the `PASSWORD` global constant, and all `IS_RELEASE` branching for page navigation have been removed. The license page now always navigates directly to the components page. The `"password"` field in `metadata.json` is simply ignored. 4. **PySide2 + PySide2-Fluent-Widgets migration** — Migrated from PySide6 (Qt 6, Windows 10+ only) to PySide2 (Qt 5, Windows 7+) for broader OS compatibility. Replaced the custom `theme.py` module with the third-party [PySide2-Fluent-Widgets](https://github.com/zhiyiYo/PyQt-Fluent-Widgets) library (`qfluentwidgets`): - All `PySide6.*` imports → `PySide2.*` in `main.py` and `uninstaller.py` - PySide6 fully-qualified enum paths → PySide2 short forms (e.g. `Qt.AlignmentFlag.AlignCenter` → `Qt.AlignCenter`, `Qt.ItemDataRole.UserRole` → `Qt.UserRole`, `Qt.CheckState.Checked` → `Qt.Checked`, etc.) - `app.exec()` → `app.exec_()` (PySide2 compatibility) - `theme.apply_theme(app)` → `setTheme(Theme.AUTO)` (auto dark/light mode via qfluentwidgets) - `setProperty("class", ...)` styling replaced with inline `setStyleSheet()` calls - `theme.py` deleted 5. **Python 3.9 downgrade** — To support older Windows environments: - All `match`/`case` statements replaced with `if`/`elif` chains (Python 3.10+ syntax) - `@override` decorator removed (Python 3.12+ feature) - CI workflows (`build-app.yml`, `build-release.yml`) changed from Python 3.13 → 3.9 - `requirements.txt` updated: PySide2 `>=5.15.0,<6` + `PySide2-Fluent-Widgets>=1.0.0`, removed PySide6/shiboken6 and packages requiring Python 3.10+, added missing `vdf` dependency 6. **x86 (32-bit) builds** — CI now uses `architecture: x86` in both workflows so the built exe runs on both 32-bit and 64-bit Windows machines (x64 can run x86 binaries natively via WoW64). 7. **README rewrite** — Full documentation: features, project structure, component config reference table, requirements (Python 3.9+, Windows 7+), usage, and uninstaller documentation. References PySide2-Fluent-Widgets for theming. 8. **Build updates** — `build.py` and both GitHub Actions workflows now build `Uninstall.exe` alongside `main.exe`. ### Bug fixes (since initial revision) - **Signal disconnect scope in `on_item_changed`** — The disconnect was incorrectly placed at the top of the entire method, which broke transitive dependency resolution (A→B→C) and incompatibility enforcement for auto-checked items. Fixed by keeping the signal connected during the dependency section and only disconnecting around the incompatibility handling and parent state update sections. - **Tar `file_type` mismatch** — All 6 locations that set `file_type = "tar.gz"` were changed to `file_type = "tar"` to match `run_extract`'s `archive_type == "tar"` check. Without this fix, tar archives were silently skipped during extraction. Also added a `"tar"` handler to `get_archive_size` (previously raised `NotImplementedError`). ### Other changes - `run_extract` now returns a list of extracted file paths (used by manifest generation) - Three helper methods added to `ComponentsPage`: `get_incompatible_ids()`, `is_disabled_by_incompatible()`, `get_component_name()` - Two new methods on `InstallThread`: `save_install_manifest()`, `copy_uninstaller()` ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [x] **PySide2 + PySide2-Fluent-Widgets x86 wheels**: CI now builds with 32-bit Python 3.9. Verify `pip install PySide2>=5.15.0,<6` and `PySide2-Fluent-Widgets>=1.0.0` install successfully with 32-bit Python 3.9 on Windows and the built exe launches without `ImportError` on both x86 and x64 machines. - [x] **PyInstaller bundling of `qfluentwidgets`**: CI runs `pyinstaller --onefile --windowed main.py` without `--hidden-import=qfluentwidgets`. Verify PyInstaller auto-detects the import. If the built exe fails with `ModuleNotFoundError`, add `--hidden-import=qfluentwidgets` to CI and `build.py`. - [x] **Inline stylesheets vs. dark/light mode**: Hardcoded inline `setStyleSheet()` colors (`#0078D4` accent, `#107C10` success, `#D13438` error, `#666666` footers) will **not** adapt to dark mode — only qfluentwidgets' own widget styling follows the system theme. Verify the visual result is acceptable in both modes. - [x] **Signal disconnect logic (transitive deps + incompatibility)**: The fix keeps the signal connected during dependency auto-checking so A→B→C works, but disconnects during incompatibility handling and parent state updates. Test with components that have both transitive dependencies and incompatibilities to verify no edge cases remain. - [ ] **Manual end-to-end test recommended**: (1) Build on 32-bit Python 3.9 + PySide2 on Windows. (2) Run the installer: test component selection, incompatibility popups, dependency auto-check. (3) Verify `install_manifest.json` and `Uninstall.exe` are created. (4) Run `Uninstall.exe` and verify component removal + registry cleanup. (5) Test on both 32-bit and 64-bit Windows if possible. ### Notes - No existing components in `items.json` use `incompatible` yet — the logic is purely additive. - This is a Windows-only GUI app; all changes were syntax-checked (`py_compile`) but not functionally tested (developed on Linux). - The `"password"` key still exists in `metadata.json` but is no longer read. It can be removed at any time. - `darkdetect` is still in `requirements.txt` — it may be unnecessary now that qfluentwidgets handles theme detection via `setTheme(Theme.AUTO)`. Consider removing it. - **Asymmetric incompatibility**: `get_incompatible_ids()` reads only a component's own `incompatible` list. If A lists B but B doesn't list A, checking B won't disable A. `is_disabled_by_incompatible()` checks both directions. Verify this asymmetry is acceptable or add B→A to both lists. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/cd5be22381f346ac8e5e27d19cf272f2 Requested by: @Aruvelut-123 ---

title: Upgrade dlib bin
body:
1. **Migrated packaging to `pyproject.toml`**  
   - Replaced `setup.py` with a modern `hatchling` build backend

2. **Updated Python requirement**  
   - Bumped `requires-python` from `>=3.8` to `>=3.9` to align with `numpy>=2`

3. **Relaxed dependency pinning**  
   - Changed `dlib-bin` and `gdown` from `==` to `>=`

4. **Removed `gdown` dependency**  
   - Replaced with `requests`, eliminating 7 transitive dependencies

5. **Modernized CI workflow**  
   - Updated Python matrix to `3.10–3.13`  
   - Switched from `pip/venv` to `uv`  
   - Replaced `setup-python` with `setup-uv`

6. **Cleaned up build artifacts**  
   - Removed `build/`, `dist/`, and `dlib_wrapper.egg-info/`

7. **Added test coverage**  
   - Assertion checks for counts  
   - Verified 128-dimensional encoding shape  
   - Ensured pipeline consistency and determinism  
   - Added handling tests for images with no detectable faces

title: Modernize for current Python (3.9+), drop legacy deps
body:
## Summary
- Remove `fabulous`, `future`, and `pillow` dependencies — now runs on the Python standard library only
- Add `compat.py` as a lightweight replacement for fabulous (ANSI colors, terminal info, text rendering)
- Drop Python 2 support, require Python 3.9+
- Add `pyproject.toml` and `__main__.py` for modern packaging (`python -m terminal_quest`)
- Fix level 4 directory reference bug, replace `old_div` with `//`, stabilize `random.sample` call
- Update README with current installation instructions

## Test plan
- [ ] Run `python -m terminal_quest` and play through all 10 levels
- [ ] Verify banner and color output display correctly in terminal
- [ ] Test `pip install .` in a fresh venv on Python 3.9+
- [ ] Confirm no third-party packages are required

title: fix: Intel GPU Dockerfile Python 3.12 compatibility
body:
## Problem

The `Dockerfile.gpu-intel` fails to build on the `intel/oneapi-runtime:latest` base image (Ubuntu Noble, Python 3.12) due to three issues:

### 1. Broken apt dependencies
`intel-level-zero-gpu` has unmet dependencies (`libigc1`, `libigdfcl1`) that are not installable. These packages (`intel-opencl-icd`, `intel-level-zero-gpu`, `level-zero`) are already present in the `intel/oneapi-runtime` base image.

### 2. Python 3.12 pkgutil.ImpImporter removal
`numpy==1.24.0` and old `setuptools` use `pkgutil.ImpImporter` which was [removed in Python 3.12](https://docs.python.org/3.12/whatsnew/3.12.html#removed). This causes:
```
AttributeError: module 'pkgutil' has no attribute 'ImpImporter'. Did you mean: 'zipimporter'?
```

### 3. GPU acceleration display
`inference.py` checks `torch.cuda.is_available()` for the GPU acceleration log message, which returns `False` on Intel XPU even though the GPU is being used.

## Changes

- **Dockerfile.gpu-intel**: Remove redundant Intel GPU packages from apt install
- **Dockerfile.gpu-intel**: Add `pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel` before torch
- **requirements.txt**: Bump numpy from `==1.24.0` to `>=1.26.0`
- **tts_engine/inference.py**: Use `is_gpu(device)` instead of `torch.cuda.is_available()`

## Testing

Tested on Intel Arc B580 (12GB VRAM) running on Unraid 7.1.4:
- Docker image builds successfully ✅
- SNAC model loads on `xpu:0` ✅
- TTS generation produces valid 24kHz WAV audio ✅
- `device.py` detects Intel XPU correctly ✅
- llama.cpp SYCL inference: ~20 tok/s ✅

title: Python 3 modernizations, optional unrar support, and added minimal Comic metadata Web UI
body:
### Motivation

- Bring the codebase up-to-date for Python 3 compatibility and make external dependencies optional so the library is more robust across environments. 
- Add a small built-in web UI to enable basic comic metadata read/write/scan workflows and ComicVine lookup. 

### Description

- Updated import paths to use package-relative imports and modernized type checks and exceptions (e.g. `isinstance`, `DeprecationWarning(...)`).
- Made UnRAR/unrar-related code more Python 3 friendly by decoding `bytes` outputs, replacing `source.next()` with `next(source)`, returning `bytes` for binary reads, and using `io.BytesIO` for image handling.
- Made optional dependencies safe: added try/except fallbacks for `natsort`, `unrar`/`unrarlib`/`constants`, and `PyPDF2`, and guarded features (e.g. RAR/PDF handling) when those libraries are absent.
- Converted the `UnRAR2` tests to use `pytest` and added a module-level skip if a system `rar`/`unrar` executable is not present.
- Modernized various Python 2 idioms to Python 3 equivalents (`print` function, `unicode`/`str` handling, `urllib.parse`, and small API surface fixes).
- Added a minimal `webui` package with a `ComicVineClient` and an HTTP server (`webui/server.py`) exposing endpoints to scan libraries, read/write metadata, and query ComicVine; included a short `WEBUI.md` usage note.

### Testing

- Ran the UnRAR integration tests via `pytest UnRAR2/test_UnRAR2.py`, which will `pytest.skip` at module level when `unrar`/`rar` is not installed, and reported no failures on environments with an unpacker available. 
- Exercised basic functionality manually via the included doctest invocation (`doctest.testmod(UnRAR2)`) as part of the UnRAR test script; no errors were reported when prerequisites were satisfied. 
- The new web UI and ComicVine client include runtime checks (requiring a `COMICVINE_API_KEY` for ComicVine endpoints) and have been exercised locally with the `webui.server.run()` entrypoint in development.

title: fix: Python 3.9 compatibility for carl-hook.py
body:
## Summary

Fixes #2 — `carl-hook.py` crashes on Python 3.9 (the default macOS system Python) due to [PEP 604](https://peps.python.org/pep-0604/) union type syntax (`X | None`), which requires Python 3.10+.

**Error on every prompt:**
```
TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for |: 'type' and 'NoneType'
```

## Changes

- Add `from typing import Optional` import
- Replace all 10 instances of `X | None` with `Optional[X]` (compatible with Python 3.7+)

No behavioral changes — type hints only.

## Test plan

- [x] Verified `python3 carl-hook.py` runs without error on Python 3.9.6
- [x] Verified hook produces correct JSON output with valid CARL rules injection
- [x] No remaining `| None` patterns in codebase

title: feat: add DiagnosticConfigManifest and install ingestion dependencies…
body:
… (#1)

* feat: add DiagnosticConfigManifest and install ingestion dependencies

* Added configuration struct `DiagnosticConfigManifest` using Pydantic BaseSettings to handle direct zip fallback and CMS endpoint base urls.
* Added `dlt`, `requests`, `beautifulsoup4`, `psycopg2-binary`, `dbt-postgres` and related `pydantic-settings` to `pyproject.toml`.
* Fully tested the new config component (100% test coverage).
* Fixed all pre-commit hooks, added `pydantic-settings` to mypy's additional dependencies, and correctly declared runtime variables to suppress deptry errors.

* fix: constraint dbt-postgres dependencies for py3.14

* `dbt-extractor=0.6.0` fails to build from source on python 3.14 and `astral-sh/uv` fails when `[project]` is missing `version`.
* Switched to `dbt-extractor==0.5.1` and `dbt-postgres==1.8.2` which have python 3.14 compatible builds.
* Ensured `psycopg2-binary` avoids local compilation with `pg_config`.

* fix: temporarily remove dbt dependencies for python 3.14 CI checks

* Removed `dbt-postgres` and `dbt-extractor` due to upstream incompatibilities with Python 3.14 that prevent installation from succeeding (`dbt-extractor` requires a build from source but its `pyproject.toml` is invalid for modern build systems like `uv`).
* These tools can be added later once Python 3.14 wheels are shipped, or when the dbt portion is implemented via subprocesses / specialized docker environments.
* Preserved `psycopg2-binary`, `dlt`, and configuration manifest.

* fix: remove dlt and psycopg2 for py3.14t build failures

* `dlt` depends on `orjson` which explicitly fails compilation on python 3.14t (freethreading) because it doesn't support the free-threaded GIL architecture.
* `psycopg2-binary` lacks wheels for python 3.14 on windows/macos and tries to compile from source but missing `pg_config`.
* Removed these upstream dev dependencies from `pyproject.toml` so CI passes. We will install the downstream data packages in a different way or in the later units once Python 3.14 is stabilized.

title: fix: upgrade chromadb to >=1.0 for NumPy 2.0 and Python 3.13 compat
body:
## Summary

Fixes `AttributeError: np.float_ was removed in the NumPy 2.0 release` crash that occurred when running `graphmy index` on Python 3.12+ / Python 3.13 environments.

### Root cause

`chromadb<=0.5.22` referenced `np.float_` in `chromadb/api/types.py` which was removed in NumPy 2.0. Python 3.13 ships with NumPy 2.x by default in most environments.

### Changes

- Upgrade `chromadb` from `>=0.4,<0.5` to `>=1.0` (NumPy 2.0-compatible)
- Keep `onnxruntime>=1.14,<1.24; python_version < '3.11'` — onnxruntime 1.24+ still has no cp310 wheel
- Add `Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13` to PyPI classifiers
- Bump version `0.1.0` → `0.1.1`
- Update `CHANGELOG.md`

### Testing

All 122 tests pass with `chromadb>=1.0`.

title: feat: build cyclonedds 0.10.5 from source, support Python 3.10+
body:
Build cyclonedds 0.10.5 from source during install, removing the dependency on third-party pre-built wheels. The cyclonedds version is locked to 0.10.5, matching the official unitree SDK.

## Changes

- **cyclonedds C library** (`thirdparty/cyclonedds/`) and **Python bindings** (`thirdparty/cyclonedds-python/`) added as git submodules (tag 0.10.5)
- Custom `setup.py` with `build_ext` that automatically:
  1. Finds C source from submodule, or downloads 0.10.5 tarball from GitHub as fallback
  2. Finds Python bindings from submodule, or downloads sdist from PyPI as fallback
  3. Builds C library with cmake
  4. Compiles `_clayer` Python C extension
  5. Bundles `libddsc.so` into the wheel
- `cyclonedds` is no longer a pip dependency — it's built from source and bundled into the package
- `requires-python` lowered from `>=3.11` to `>=3.10`
- Removed dependency on carlosdp's custom pre-built wheels

## Usage in other projects

```toml
[project]
dependencies = ["unitree-sdk2py"]

[tool.uv.sources]
unitree-sdk2py = { git = "https://github.com/MiaoDX-fork-and-pruning/unitree_sdk2_python_uv" }
```

`uv sync` automatically downloads and builds cyclonedds from source — no extra steps needed.

## Prerequisites

- cmake, C compiler (gcc/clang)
- Python >= 3.10

title: Implement Week 2 synthetic generation, runner workflow, and golden sets
body:
## Summary
This PR implements **Week 2 (Days 6-10)** from `docs/01-conversion-validator-spec.md`:

- Day 6: synthetic expression generation
- Day 7: synthetic pipeline generation
- Day 8: ground-truth suite generation + converter evaluation
- Day 9: runner workflow for 50-100 synthetic pipeline evaluation + CCS distribution + triage
- Day 10: golden set materialization (`golden_sets/expressions.json`, `golden_sets/pipelines.json`)

It also includes alignment fixes so synthetic expected outputs match current `wkmigrate` emitter behavior exactly.

## Scope
### New Week 2 core modules
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/synthetic/expression_generator.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/synthetic/pipeline_generator.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/synthetic/ground_truth.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/synthetic/runner.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/golden_set.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/report.py` (report/distribution support)

### New tests
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_expression_generator.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_pipeline_generator.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_ground_truth.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_runner.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_golden_set.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_day9_day10_workflow.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/__init__.py`

### Generated artifacts
- `golden_sets/expressions.json`
- `golden_sets/pipelines.json`

## wkmigrate alignment fixes included
### Expression templates
Updated all expected Python outputs to match current emitter output format from `wkmigrate.parsers.expression_functions` and expression emitter tests, including:

- `concat` as `str(a) + str(b)`
- string funcs (`toUpper`, `toLower`, `trim`, `substring`, `replace`, `indexOf`, `startsWith`, `endsWith`, `contains`)
- math/logical operators with expected parenthesization
- `div` as `int(a / b)`
- casts (`int`, `float`, `string`, `bool`)
- collection funcs (`first`, `last`, `coalesce`, `empty`)
- datetime helpers (`utcNow`, `formatDateTime`)
- removed unsupported `dayOfMonth`

Added compile-validity test for all generated `expected_python` expressions.

### Pipeline activity shape
Updated default synthetic activity types to:

- `SetVariable`, `IfCondition`, `DatabricksNotebook`, `Copy`, `Lookup`, `WebActivity`, `ForEach`

and removed `AppendVariable` from defaults.

Generated activity payloads now follow `wkmigrate`'s normalized translator input shape (snake_case keys and top-level `IfCondition.expression`).

### Mode stubs
`PipelineGenerator` now fails fast for unsupported generation modes without provider:

- raises `NotImplementedError("LLM mode requires a judge_provider")` when mode is `llm` or `adversarial` and provider is absent.

## Behavioral highlights
- `GroundTruthSuite.evaluate_converter(...)` runs converter output through `evaluate(...)` and returns aggregate `Report`.
- `run_synthetic_workflow(...)` returns:
  - aggregated `Report`
  - CCS distribution summary
  - triaged failure list (threshold + expression mismatch reasons)
- `materialize_golden_sets(...)` writes expression + pipeline golden artifacts and loaders round-trip them.

## Tests
Executed:

- `PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/synthetic -q`
- `PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -q`
- `PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v`

Result:
- **90 passed, 13 skipped**

## Review guide
1. Review `synthetic/expression_generator.py` first (template correctness vs emitter outputs).
2. Review `synthetic/pipeline_generator.py` for normalized activity JSON and mode guards.
3. Review `synthetic/ground_truth.py` and `synthetic/runner.py` for evaluation/triage flow.
4. Review `golden_set.py` + generated `golden_sets/*.json` for artifact format and loadability.
5. Review `tests/unit/synthetic/*` as acceptance criteria.

## Notes
- `golden_sets/pipelines.json` is large by design (materialized Week 2 Day 10 artifact).

title: Blender 5.1/Python 3.13 compatibility, profile migration, vendoring improvements, and Poppy Playtime profiles
body:
### Motivation
- Add compatibility with newer Blender releases (Blender 5.1 / Python 3.13) and make profile storage cross-platform and robust.
- Improve packaging/build reliability by vendoring already-installed distributions when `pip` installation into the distribution target fails.
- Support Blender's deferred property annotations so class auto-registration works with both legacy and newer annotation styles.
- Provide convenient, selectable generic game profiles for Poppy Playtime Chapters 1–5 in the add-on UI.

### Description
- Updated `README.md` and `docs/source/installation.rst` to document Blender 5.1 / Python 3.13 support and cross-platform profile storage behavior.
- Extended `build.py` with `_iter_requirements`, `_requirement_import_name`, and `_copy_installed_distribution` helper functions and changed the requirements installation loop to create `umodel_tools/third_party`, attempt `pip install` per requirement, and fall back to copying an already-installed distribution when pip fails.
- Added support in `umodel_tools/auto_load.py` to detect Blender's deferred property wrapper (`_PropertyDeferred`) and extract `PointerProperty`/`CollectionProperty` dependencies so registration ordering works with deferred annotations.
- Added five generic shim game profiles under `umodel_tools/game_profiles/` for `poppy_playtime_chapter_1` through `poppy_playtime_chapter_5` that reuse the existing `generic` handlers and provide `GAME_NAME`/`GAME_DESCRIPTION` entries.
- Changed `umodel_tools/preferences.py` to store profiles in Blender's per-user config directory via `bpy.utils.user_resource('CONFIG', ...)`, added legacy `Documents/XModel` support and automatic migration via `_migrate_legacy_profiles`, made profile loading search both new and legacy locations with deduplication, and adjusted parsing/attribute assignment accordingly.
- Minor metadata update in `umodel_tools/__init__.py` to add a `doc_url` to `bl_info`.

### Testing
- No automated tests were added or executed as part of this change.

title: Convert all Python files from Python 2 to Python 3
body: ## Summary Mechanical Python 2 → Python 3 migration across all three `.py` files. No logic or behavior changes. **Changes by file:** - **Container.py** — `print` statements → `print()` calls; `push_file` opens in `'rb'` mode and decodes base64 output to `str` (required by Py3's `base64.b64encode` which expects `bytes`) - **HPC_data_collector.py** — `print` statements → `print()` calls; bare `except:` → `except Exception:` (no longer silently swallows `KeyboardInterrupt`/`SystemExit`) - **DataParser.py** — wraps `d_data.values()` and `d_data.keys()` in `list()` since Py3 dict views are not directly accepted as positional args to `pd.DataFrame` ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **`push_file` binary read + base64 encoding**: Verify that reading `ASLRay.sh` in `'rb'` mode and calling `base64.b64encode(content).decode('ascii')` produces a valid shell-safe string when interpolated into the `echo ... | base64 -d` command inside the container. This is the only non-trivial Py3 adaptation. - [ ] **`except Exception:` narrowing**: Confirm you're okay with `is_net_on()` no longer catching `KeyboardInterrupt` and `SystemExit`. This is generally better practice but changes behavior if someone Ctrl+C's during the network check. - [ ] **End-to-end test in LXC environment**: These changes could only be syntax-checked and mock-tested — no real LXC containers were available. Run `HPC_data_collector.py` (both `aslray_mode = True` and `False`) against a live container to confirm nothing broke. ### Notes - Requested by: @Person543 - [Link to Devin session](https://app.devin.ai/sessions/95e9e385366946d99758392f8a1ee4da) ---

title: feat: add module-based piscine directory structure
body:
The repository lacked the module-organized structure matching the official 42 Python for Data Science piscine. Exercises existed only as a flat `exercises/` folder with no module separation.

## Structure added

Five module directories, each with zero-padded exercise subfolders (`ex00`–`exNN`):

```
python_0_starting/   ex00–ex09
python_1_array/      ex00–ex05
python_2_datatable/  ex00–ex04
python_3_oop/        ex00–ex04
python_4_dod/        ex00–ex02
```

## Starter files per exercise

Each exercise folder contains three files following the established repo conventions:

- **`main.py`** — module docstring, `main()` entry point, `if __name__ == "__main__":` guard
- **`README.md`** — Objective / Instructions / Requirements / Implementation Notes / Example sections
- **`notes.md`** — Key Observations / Difficulties / Ideas for Follow-up sections

## Root README

Updated to document the new module layout, explain what the piscine is, describe exercise organization, and update the `python` run path example to match the new structure.

title: fix: upgrade pydantic and openai for Python 3.13 compatibility
body:
Railway deploys on Python 3.13, which is incompatible with pydantic-core==2.16.3 (bundled with pydantic==2.6.4). The build failed because pydantic-core had no pre-built wheel for Python 3.13, forcing a Rust/maturin source compilation that crashed due to a breaking change in ForwardRef._evaluate() in Python 3.13.

Bumped versions to packages that ship pre-built py3.13 wheels:
- pydantic: 2.6.4 → 2.7.4
- openai:   1.14.0 → 1.30.0
- fastapi:  0.110.0 → 0.115.0

No source code changes required — purely a dependency version fix.

title: Fix Railway build failure and security vulnerabilities in requirements.txt
body:
Railway's default Python 3.13 runtime rejected the pinned dependencies — `numpy`, `matplotlib`, `kiwisolver`, and `contourpy` had no Python 3.13 wheels and failed to compile from source.

## Dependency upgrades

| Package | Old | New | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| `numpy` | 1.24.3 | 2.2.4 | 1.24.x tops out at Python 3.11; 2.x required for 3.13 |
| `matplotlib` | 3.7.1 | 3.9.4 | Python 3.13 wheels available from 3.8+ |
| `kiwisolver` | 1.4.4 | 1.4.8 | Python 3.13 wheels |
| `contourpy` | 1.0.7 | 1.3.1 | Python 3.13 wheels |
| `Pillow` | 9.5.0 | 12.1.1 | Python 3.13 wheels + CVE: out-of-bounds write in PSD loader |
| `fonttools` | 4.39.4 | 4.55.3 | Python 3.13 wheels |
| `Django` | 4.2.1 | 4.2.29 | CVE: SQL injection in column aliases and `_connector` kwarg; DoS in `HttpResponseRedirect` on Windows |
| `asgiref`, `cycler`, `packaging`, `pyparsing`, `sqlparse`, `tzdata` | various | bumped | Routine updates alongside above |

## New files

- **`.python-version`** — pins `3.13` so Railway/Nixpacks uses an explicit version rather than defaulting to whatever is latest at build time.

title: Migrate to GitHub Actions CI
body:
## Migrate CI from Travis CI to GitHub Actions

Travis CI was inherited from the upstream orator project and tested Python 2.7–3.7 — versions incompatible with this fork's python = "^3.10" requirement. Travis is also effectively unavailable for open-source projects. This PR replaces it with GitHub Actions.

Changes:

- Add .github/workflows/tests.yml with two jobs:
- unit-tests — runs across Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14 (no external services needed)
- integration-tests — runs across Python 3.10 and 3.12 using Docker Compose to spin up Postgres 14 and MySQL 8
- Delete .travis.yml
- Delete tox.ini (also stale, same Python 2.7–3.7 matrix)
- Fix exit-code masking in make test and make test-int — previously docker-down ran via ; which swallowed a failing pytest exit code; now the exit code is captured and re-raised after cleanup

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> ## Problem
> 
> The Streamlit Cloud deployment is failing with `installer returned a non-zero exit code` / `Error during processing dependencies!` because of invalid/non-existent package versions in `requirements.txt`.
> 
> ### Root Causes

title: Fix CI: Python 3.10 compatibility and black formatting drift
body:
Two CI failures on the `copilot/fix-module-not-found-error` branch: `datetime.UTC` (Python 3.11+) caused `ImportError` on the Python 3.10 runners, and 9 files had accumulated Black formatting drift.

## Changes

- **`datetime.UTC` → `timezone.utc`** across all affected files — `datetime.UTC` was introduced in Python 3.11; replaced with `timezone.utc` (available since Python 3.2) to restore 3.10 compatibility:
  ```python
  # Before (Python 3.11+ only)
  from datetime import UTC, datetime
  return datetime.now(UTC)

  # After
  from datetime import datetime, timezone
  return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
  ```
  Files affected: `auth/model.py`, `finance/models.py`, `finance/routes.py`, `finance/service.py`, `tests/test_finance.py`

- **Black formatting** — ran `black .` to bring all 9 drifted files into conformance with the enforced style check

title: Fix Python 2.7 virtualenv PermissionError and Passenger crash-loop lock contention
body:
Two cPanel deployment failures: (1) virtualenv creation fails with `PermissionError` on CloudLinux Python 2.7 `.pyc` files; (2) any startup exception puts Passenger into a crash-restart loop, blocking cPanel's app management lock and causing `Can't acquire lock for app: FakeNTP-cpanel` on operations like **Run Script**.

## Python version enforcement

- `passenger_wsgi.py` / `cli.py` — fail-fast guard before any imports:
  ```python
  if sys.version_info < (3, 8):
      raise RuntimeError("FakeNTP requires Python 3.8 or later. ...")
  ```
- `pyproject.toml` — added `requires-python = ">=3.8"` under a `[project]` section

## Passenger crash-loop prevention

Wraps `create_app()` in `passenger_wsgi.py` with a `try/except` that keeps Passenger alive on any startup failure by serving a `500` fallback instead of crashing:

```python
try:
    from fakentp.app import create_app
    application = create_app()
except Exception as _exc:
    _log.critical("FakeNTP failed to initialize: %s", _exc, exc_info=True)
    _msg = ("FakeNTP failed to start: {}\n"
            "Check the server error log for details.".format(_exc)).encode()

    def application(environ, start_response):
        start_response("500 Internal Server Error", [...])
        return [_msg]
```

This stops the restart loop so cPanel can acquire the management lock for **Run Script** / package installs even when the app is misconfigured.

## Documentation

Added a **Troubleshooting** section to `README.md` covering both errors with cause and fix steps.

- graph/road/frame compatibility fixes,

### Graph and frame pipeline migration
- Refactored graph wrappers for current `iduedu` API in `transport_frames/graph.py`.
- Added/updated support for edge category mapping to legacy `reg`.

  - robust handling of `index_right`/`index_right0` from `GeoPandas` joins.

  - added robust `sjoin` right-index column detection,
  - improved handling of road visualization when `properties` column is absent.
- Replaced deprecated geometry union usage where needed.

### Release automation

  - uses `PYPI_API_TOKEN` repository secret.

## Notes
- Intended target release: **v2.0.6**.

title: Fix dependency pins to unblock Streamlit Cloud deployment on Python 3.14
body:
Two hard install failures prevented the app from deploying on Streamlit Cloud's Python 3.14 runtime:

- `pandas==2.1.4` — Cython extensions fail to compile against Python 3.14's updated `_PyLong_AsByteArray` C API
- `scikit-learn==1.4.2` — pulls in `numpy==2.0.0rc1` as a build dep, which was never published to PyPI

## Changes

- **`requirements.txt`**: Loosened `==` pins to `>=` so pip resolves pre-built wheels instead of triggering source builds
  - `pandas==2.1.4` → `>=2.2.3` (first release with Python 3.12+ wheels)
  - `scikit-learn==1.4.2` → `>=1.5.2` (drops broken `numpy==2.0.0rc1` build dep)
  - `numpy==1.26.4` → `>=2.1.0` (aligns with updated pandas/scikit-learn requirements)

title: Update torch support
body:
This pull request updates the GitHub Actions CI workflows to support newer versions of `torchaudio` and expands the workflow triggers to run on all branches, not just `main`. The changes also update the Python version matrix and simplify the configuration.

**Workflow trigger updates:**
* Both `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and `.github/workflows/espnet.yml` now trigger on pushes to any branch, not just `main`, in addition to pull requests. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b803fcb7f17ed9235f1e5cb1fcd2f5d3b2838429d4368ae4c57ce4436577f03fL4-L7) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d262d454dcd9ed72c82352c952d29a7712cfe4f3f351345e01e5424e88fa9651L4-L7)

**Dependency and matrix updates:**
* The `torchaudio-version` matrix in both workflows is updated to test only recent versions: `2.5.1`, `2.7.1`, `2.8.0`, and `2.9.1`, removing older versions. title: Modernize project: migrate to Python 3.9+, Poetry, and updated tooling
body:
This pull request introduces significant modernization and cleanup to the project, shifting its focus to Python 3.9+ and updating tooling, documentation, and project metadata. The changes remove legacy files and configurations, update documentation and references to the new repository and package name, and transition the build and dependency management to use Poetry. The project now targets modern Python, improves maintainability, and streamlines development workflows.

**Project Modernization and Tooling:**
- Switched dependency and build management from manual requirements and Makefile scripts to Poetry, updating `Makefile` commands accordingly and removing `dev_requirements.txt`. title: Migrate Douban crawler from MySQL to SQLite and modernize to Python 3
body:
### Motivation
- Reduce runtime dependency maintenance by replacing MySQL with a local SQLite store.
- Make the script runnable under modern Python by converting legacy Python 2 constructs to Python 3.
- Update the Douban login endpoint in case the service entrypoint changed and keep the login flow workable.

### Description
- Replaced `MySQLdb` usage with built-in `sqlite3`, added `db_path = "douban.sqlite3"`, and implemented `_init_db()` to create the `comments` table automatically.
- Rewrote `crawl_comments` and `post_comment` to use parameterized SQLite queries and local DB operations, including clearing the table and resetting the sequence before crawl.
- Modernized Python syntax and libraries: switched to `#!/usr/bin/env python3`, `print()`/`input()`, `http.cookiejar`, explicit HTML parser `BeautifulSoup(..., "html.parser")`, and fixed header keys (e.g., `Connection`).
- Updated `domain` and `login_url` to `https://www.douban.com` and `https://accounts.douban.com/passport/login`, and refreshed `README.md` with Python 3 + SQLite instructions and a quick `py_compile` check.

### Testing
- `python3 -m py_compile douban_crawler.py` passes after the refactor.
- An import smoke test (`from douban_crawler import DoubanSpider`) failed in the environment due to missing third-party dependency `requests` and not due to syntax errors.
- The original Python 2 script previously failed `py_compile` on Python 3 with `print` syntax errors, which are resolved by this change.

title: Fix LangChain import failure in retrieval pipeline and add weighted ensemble fusion
body:
### Motivation
- The retrieval pipeline failed at startup due to a missing `langchain.retrievers.EnsembleRetriever` import in newer LangChain installs, and the change aims to remove that dependency and stabilize compatibility. 
- Also provide a configurable, weighted fusion for hybrid retrievals and make the project dependency constraints clearer to avoid runtime incompatibilities.

### Description
- Removed use / import of `langchain.retrievers.EnsembleRetriever` and reworked `hybrid_ensemble` to fetch BM25 and vector results separately and fuse them with an in-repo weighted reciprocal-rank fusion in `src/retrieval/pipeline.py`.
- Extended `reciprocal_rank_fusion` in `src/retrieval/fusion.py` to accept `weights`, normalize them safely via a new `_normalize_weights` helper, and apply weights when scoring fused results.
- Pinned `langchain` in `requirements.txt` to `langchain>=0.3.0,<1.0.0` to avoid breaking API changes from LangChain v1+.
- Added a README note to recommend Python 3.11–3.13 because some LangChain dependencies are not compatible with Python 3.14.

### Testing
- Ran unit tests with `python -m pytest -q`, which returned `2 passed`.
- Compiled modified modules with `python -m compileall src/retrieval/pipeline.py src/retrieval/fusion.py`, which completed successfully.

```pytb
❯ python --version
Python 3.12.4
❯ python -X importtime -c 'import pprint' 2> import.log && tuna import.log -o /tmp/tuna-html
Exception in thread Thread-1 (<lambda>):
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12/threading.py", line 1073, in _bootstrap_inner
    self.run()
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12/threading.py", line 1010, in run
    self._target(*self._args, **self._kwargs)
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12/site-packages/tuna/cli.py", line 29, in <lambda>
    target=lambda: webbrowser.open_new_tab(outdir / "index.html")
                   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12/webbrowser.py", line 103, in open_new_tab
    return open(url, 2)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12/webbrowser.py", line 87, in open
    if browser.open(url, new, autoraise):
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12/webbrowser.py", line 639, in open
    script = 'open location "%s"' % url.replace('"', '%22') # opens in default browser
```

title: fix: resolve Dependabot security alerts in dev-requirements.txt
body:
## Summary

Resolves all 43 open Dependabot security alerts by updating vulnerable dependency version constraints in \dev-requirements.txt\.

## Changes

### Pillow
- **Python <=3.7**: \6.2.0\ → \9.5.0\ (last version to support Python 3.7)
  - Fixes ~20 high/critical CVEs across Pillow 6.x–9.x
- **Python ==3.8**: loosened pin from \==10.3.0\ to \>=10.3.0\ (Pillow 11+ dropped Python 3.8; 10.3.0 remains the highest safe version)
  - No functional change, just ensures pip resolves to a non-vulnerable release
- **Python >3.8**: already at \12.1.1\ — no change needed

### pywin32 (alert #16)
- Python <=3.6 line: changed from \<=227\ (vulnerable) to \>=301\
- Python >3.6 line: bumped minimum from \>=300\ to \>=301\
- Fixes integer overflow CVE resolved in pywin32 301

## Testing
No code changes — only version constraints updated. CI (AppVeyor) will validate across all supported Python versions.

title: Refactor for Python 3.14 / PyQuery 2.0: replace URL-fetching pq(url) with requests
body: PyQuery 2.0 removed implicit URL fetching — `pq(url)` now wraps the URL string as HTML content instead of making an HTTP request. This broke all URL-fetching paths across the codebase, causing functions like `_retrieve_html_page` to return `[url...]` instead of `None` on invalid URLs. ## Production code changes - **All `pq(url)` URL-fetch calls replaced** across boxscores, rosters, rankings, conferences, schedules, and `fb/` modules: ```python # Before try: url_data = pq(url) except HTTPError: return None # After try: response = requests.get(url) response.raise_for_status() url_data = pq(response.text) except requests.exceptions.RequestException: return None ``` - **`urllib.error.HTTPError` imports removed** and replaced with `import requests` throughout; exception handling updated to `requests.exceptions.RequestException` - **`utils._pull_page`** updated to use `requests.get` + `raise_for_status()` - **NBA 2021 season fallback logic** in `nba_utils.py`, `nba/roster.py`, and `nba/schedule.py` updated — these only needed to probe whether the URL exists, so `doc` is intentionally not assigned in the probe block (it's fetched again with the corrected `year` immediately after) ## Test changes - Added `raise_for_status()` to all `MockPQ` classes so they properly simulate `requests.Response`: ```python def raise_for_status(self): if self.status_code >= 400: import requests raise requests.exceptions.HTTPError(response=self) ``` ## Pre-existing lint bugs fixed - Replaced `type(x) == dict` / `type(x) == str` with `isinstance()` in player files and `fb_utils.py` (E721)

title: prueba2
Update dependencies for Python 3.13 compatibility
body:
## Problem

The build fails on Python 3.13 because `pydantic==2.5.0` attempts to compile pydantic-core from source and hits a `TypeError: ForwardRef._evaluate() missing 1 required keyword-only argument: 'recursive_guard'` — a known incompatibility between old pydantic versions and Python 3.13's updated typing internals. `fastapi==0.104.1` and `uvicorn==0.24.0` are similarly too old to have pre-built wheels for Python 3.13.

## Solution

Relaxed the three pinned versions in `requirements.txt` to minimum bounds that ship Python 3.13-compatible wheels: `pydantic>=2.7.0`, `fastapi>=0.109.0`, and `uvicorn>=0.27.0`. The remaining dependencies are unaffected and keep their existing pins.

### Changes
- **Modified** `requirements.txt`

title: Fix Python 3.13 compatibility in requirements.txt
body:
## Problem

The build fails with "the configured Python interpreter version (3.13) is newer than PyO3's maximum supported version (3.12)". `fastapi==0.104.1` pulls in `pydantic-core==2.18.4`, which is built with PyO3 0.21.2 — a version that only supports up to Python 3.12.

## Solution

Upgraded `fastapi` to `0.115.0` and pinned `pydantic` to `2.9.2`, which pulls in `pydantic-core>=2.23.4`. That release uses PyO3 0.22+, which fully supports Python 3.13. Also bumped `uvicorn`, `httpx`, `typing-extensions`, and `urllib3` to contemporaneous stable versions, and removed the duplicate `asyncio` entry.

### Changes
- **Modified** `requirements.txt`

title: Upgrade to Django 3.x and Python 3.10+
body:
Modernizes django-form-utils to support Django 3.0-3.2 and Python 3.6+. Drops support for Django < 2.2 and Python 2.x.

## Core Changes

**Removed Python 2 compatibility layer:**
- `django.utils.six` → native Python 3 (`six.text_type()` → `str()`, `six.iteritems()` → `.items()`)
- `@python_2_unicode_compatible` decorator removed

**Fixed Django 3.x compatibility:**
- `BoundField` import: `django.forms.forms.BoundField` → `django.forms.boundfield.BoundField`
- Widget `render()` methods now accept `renderer` parameter
- Template tags use dict context instead of `template.Context`

**Rewrote `ClearableFileInput` widget rendering:**
Django 3.x moved from `format_output()` to template-based widget rendering. Overrode `render()` to maintain custom template formatting:

```python
def render(self, name, value, attrs=None, renderer=None):
    # Decompress and render subwidgets individually
    rendered_widgets = []
    for i, widget in enumerate(self.widgets):
        widget_name = '%s_%s' % (name, i)
        rendered_widgets.append(widget.render(widget_name, value[i], ...))
    
    # Apply custom template
    if current_value:
        return mark_safe(self.template % {'input': rendered_widgets[0], 'checkbox': rendered_widgets[1]})
    return mark_safe(rendered_widgets[0])
```

## Configuration Updates

- `setup.py`: Python 3.6-3.10, Django 2.2-3.2
- `tox.ini`: Updated test matrix, removed South dependency
- Test runner: `MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES` → `MIDDLEWARE`, added `TEMPLATES` configuration

## Test Updates

Accounts for Django 3.x behavioral changes:
- HTML output includes `required` attributes
- Field widgets are deep-copied (identity checks replaced with type checks)

All 48 tests passing on Django 3.0, 3.1, 3.2.

title: Fix for Empty except
body:
To fix the problem, we should (1) use correct Python 3 exception syntax and (2) avoid an empty `except` that only `pass`-es without explanation. The minimal behavior-preserving fix is to catch `(TypeError, ValueError)` and either log the error or, if logging is considered too noisy, add a clear comment explaining why errors are intentionally ignored. Since we cannot assume an existing logging setup, but PyQt applications commonly print or use `QMessageBox` selectively, the least invasive and still CodeQL-acceptable approach is to keep ignoring the error but add an explanatory comment and use the tuple exception syntax. That way, malformed breadth data from `QSettings` is skipped explicitly and the rest of the app continues unchanged.

Concretely, in `app/main_window.py` around lines 621–624, replace:

```py
                try:
                    self._col_widths[tab] = [int(w) for w in raw]
                except TypeError, ValueError:
                    pass
```

with:

```py
                try:
                    self._col_widths[tab] = [int(w) for w in raw]
                except (TypeError, ValueError):
                    # Ignore malformed or non-integer column width settings and fall back to defaults.
                    pass
```

This keeps all existing functionality (bad settings are ignored) while satisfying the static analysis rule and fixing the Python 3 exception syntax.

title: Fix CI #16: resolve pylint violations and Python 3.13 compatibility

CI #16 failed due to pylint violations across 6 files (exit code 1, 9.46/10 score) and Python 3.13 version constraint mismatch. All issues are resolved without disabling pylint globally.

### Deferred imports moved to module top-level

### Inline pylint disables for intentional patterns

- `too-few-public-methods` (R0903) on `Meta` inner classes in `jobs.py` and test class in `test_zabbix_diff.py`

### Test cleanup

`psycopg2-binary` lacks Python 3.13 support, breaking Streamlit Cloud deployments.

## Changes

**Dependencies**
- `requirements.txt`: Replace `psycopg2-binary==2.9.9` → `psycopg[binary]==3.1.18`

**SQLAlchemy Dialect**
- `backend/app/core/config.py`: Update connection URL to use explicit driver dialect
```python
# Before
return f"postgresql://{self.POSTGRES_USER}:..."

# After  
return f"postgresql+psycopg://{self.POSTGRES_USER}:..."
```

**Import Path**
- `backend/app/db/database.py`: Fix relative import `from .config` → `from ..core.config`

**Docker Images**
- `deployment/docker/Dockerfile.{backend,frontend}`: Bump base image `python:3.11-slim` → `python:3.13-slim`

**Documentation**
- `README.md`: Note Python 3.13 support, update setup instructions

## Compatibility

Maintains backward compatibility with Python 3.11+. No API changes.

title: Fix: Downgrade Python to 3.12 for pydantic compatibility
body:
## Problem

The build fails during dependency installation because Railpack defaults to Python 3.13, which is incompatible with pydantic-core 2.18.4 — a transitive dependency of aiogram 3.7.0. PyO3, used to build pydantic-core's native extension, only supports up to Python 3.12, producing the error: "the configured Python interpreter version (3.13) is newer than PyO3's maximum supported version (3.12)".

## Solution

Added a railpack.toml at the repository root with `python = "3.12"` under the `[build]` section. This explicitly instructs Railpack to use Python 3.12 instead of the default 3.13, which is fully compatible with pydantic-core and all other aiogram dependencies.

### Changes
- **Created** `railpack.toml`

---

title: Rewrite VM: Frame-based architecture, O(1) stack, 3.11-only, 41 differential tests
body:
Major architectural refactoring of the Python bytecode interpreter:

Stack discipline: Flip TOS from index 0 to index -1. All stack ops
are now O(1) — append/pop instead of insert(0)/pop(0). Fixes pre-existing
unary op stack leak (insert without pop).

Frame-based execution: Introduce Frame class with explicit frame stack.
CALL pushes a new Frame, RETURN_VALUE pops and resumes caller. Eliminates
host recursion for guest calls — guest call depth is no longer bounded by
Python's recursion limit.

VMFunction: Replace _mf_make() closure factory with VMFunction class that
stores code + defaults + closure metadata without creating a host callable.

Index-based fast locals: Frame.locals_fast is a list indexed by co_varnames
slot. LOAD_FAST/STORE_FAST use direct indexing, no name lookups.

IR via dis.get_instructions: Replace manual bytecode_optimize() with
dis.get_instructions(adaptive=False, show_caches=False). Handles
EXTENDED_ARG, CACHE slots, and jump target resolution automatically.

Correct 3.11 CALL protocol: _NULL sentinel (distinct from None) for
PUSH_NULL. CALL checks PEEK(argc+2) to determine method vs non-method
calls, matching CPython's ceval.c behavior.

Drop Python 2.7: Remove hasarg(), variable-width bytecode path, and all
2.7-only opcodes (PRINT_ITEM, SETUP_LOOP, CALL_FUNCTION variants, etc).

Exception handling: General try/except around opcode dispatch routes
exceptions through the 3.11 exception table.

Remove meta-circular hacks: No more py2vm name patching in STORE_NAME
or CALL. VMFunction/native callable distinction makes this unnecessary.

New opcodes: BUILD_SET, END_FOR, LIST_EXTEND, SET_UPDATE, SET_ADD,
LIST_TO_TUPLE, RAISE_VARARGS, BEFORE_WITH, DELETE_ATTR, DELETE_GLOBAL,
IMPORT_NAME, IMPORT_FROM, IMPORT_STAR. Tier 6 (generators/async) rejected
with NotImplementedError.

Testing: Add test_vm.py differential harness (41 tests) comparing VM
stdout against CPython across Tiers 1-5 — arithmetic, functions, closures,
classes, exceptions, comprehensions, imports, f-strings, etc.

title: Handle floating RRULE UNTIL for local calendar events
body:
### Motivation
- When converting event `DTSTART`/`DTEND` datetimes to floating local time in `_parse_event`, a timezone-aware `RRULE` `UNTIL` can remain timezone-aware and cause naive/aware comparison errors in `dateutil` during recurrence expansion, so `UNTIL` must be normalized to match.

### Description
- Added logic in `_parse_event` to detect when `EVENT_RRULE` is a `Recur` and convert a timezone-aware `rrule.until` to a floating local datetime using `dt_util.as_local(...).replace(tzinfo=None)`.
- Added an inline comment explaining why `UNTIL` must be made floating to match a floating `DTSTART`.

### Testing
- Ran `python -m compileall homeassistant/components/local_calendar/calendar.py`, which could not complete in this environment because the pinned `pyenv` Python `3.14.2` is not installed, so compilation could not be verified.
- No other automated tests were executed in this environment.

title: Complete Python 3 migration and update build tooling to uv
body:
## Changes

- **Python 3 Migration**: Updated codebase for Python 3.12+ compatibility, including:
  - Fixed file object handling by separating read and write file descriptors (`rfd`/`wfd` and `rfile`/`wfile`)
  - Fixed dict iteration with `list(dict.items())` for Python 3 compatibility

- **Build Tooling**: Updated to use `uv` for dependency management and running commands
  - Added `.gitignore` for `.claude/` and `.venv/` directories
  - Updated README with `uv sync` setup instructions
  - Replaced all `python` commands with `uv run python` in examples

- **Documentation**: Updated README to reflect Python 3.12+ requirements and modern dependency versions (gevent 26.x, greenlet 3.x)

title: Port Drupalgeddon.py to Python 3
body:
### Motivation
- Make the legacy `Drupalgeddon.py` script run under Python 3 by replacing Python 2-only APIs and addressing bytes/str differences used during hashing and HTTP requests.
- Fix password hash generation and base64 conversion so the Drupal password hashing helper works correctly with Python 3 byte digests.

### Description
- Updated shebang to `#!/usr/bin/env python3` and replaced `urllib2` with `urllib.request` and `urllib.error` for HTTP operations.
- Converted the `DrupalHash` helper to explicitly encode strings to bytes before hashing and adjusted the `custom64` routine to operate on byte values instead of using `ord` on str.
- Encoded the POST payload with `data=...encode('utf-8')`, decode HTTP responses safely with `.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')`, and modernized print statements to Python 3 syntax.
- Minor refactors and naming cleanups including renaming `hash` to `password_hash`, `UA` to `ua`, and using `.format()` for the `post_data` construction.

### Testing
- Ran `python3 -m py_compile Drupalgeddon.py` which completed successfully.
- Executed `python3 Drupalgeddon.py` which ran under Python 3 and displayed usage/banner output as expected.

title: Bump python-dotenv from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2
body:
Bumps [python-dotenv](https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2.
Release notes

Sourced from python-dotenv's releases.

v1.2.2

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by @​bbc2 in #790c5
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by @​JYOuyang in theskumar/python-dotenv#590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Misc

  • skip 000 permission tests for root user by @​burnout-projects in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • Bump actions/checkout from 5 to 6 in the github-actions group by @​dependabot[bot] in theskumar/python-dotenv#593
  • Add Windows testing to CI by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#604
  • Improve workflow efficiency with best practices by @​theskumar in theskumar/python-dotenv#609
  • Remove the use of sh in tests by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#612

New Contributors

  • @​JYOuyang made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#590
  • @​burnout-projects made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • @​cpackham-atlnz made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#597

Full Changelog: https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/compare/v1.2.1...v1.2.2

Changelog

Sourced from python-dotenv's changelog.

[1.2.2] - 2026-03-01

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#588)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by [@​bbc2] in #607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Dropped Support for Python 3.9.

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

title: feat: CRUD for documents, components, milestones, and templates
body:
## Summary

Extends the CLI with four new entity types, each with its own storage quirks.

- `huly documents list/get/create/update/describe` — documents live in teamspaces (not projects); content is a blob ref accessed via Collaborator RPC with field `"content"`
- `huly components list/get/create/update` — project-scoped, optional `--lead` with fuzzy person match
- `huly milestones list/get/create/update` — project-scoped, `--target-date YYYY-MM-DD` parsing, status enum (planned/in-progress/completed)
- `huly templates list/get/describe` — descriptions are inline ProseMirror JSON (not blob refs), so reads/writes go through `TxUpdateDoc` directly; safety check prevents corruption if a blob ref is detected
- Collaborator RPC generalized to `get_content(class_id, object_id, field, blob_ref)` — works for any entity, not just issues

## Context

- Template `describe --set` includes a safety check: if the description field looks like a blob ref (`*-description-*` pattern), it aborts rather than corrupting data by overwriting the ref with raw JSON
- `get_description`/`set_description` remain as convenience wrappers for backward compatibility
- Components and milestones are currently empty in the EFS workspace but the API classes are verified queryable

title: Migrate clean.py to Python 3
body:
`clean.py` used Python 2-only syntax, making it incompatible with Python 3.

## Changes

- **`raw_input` → `input`**: Python 3 removed `raw_input`
- **`print` statements → `print()` calls**: Python 3 print function syntax
- **Fix regex escape sequence**: Changed string literal to raw string (`r"..."`) to eliminate `SyntaxWarning` on `\[` in the pattern

```python
# Before
ans = raw_input("remove %s ([y]/n)? " % img)
print "all images in latex: ", len(all_used_img)
regex_string = "\\includegraphics(?:\[.*?\])?\{(.+?)\}"

# After
ans = input("remove %s ([y]/n)? " % img)
print("all images in latex: ", len(all_used_img))
regex_string = r"\\includegraphics(?:\[.*?\])?\{(.+?)\}"
```

title: Migrate codebase from Python 2 to Python 3
body:
Both `main.py` and `experimental.py` used Python 2-only constructs (`izip`, `print` statements, `raw_input`, unguarded `map()`). This migrates the codebase to Python 3.

### `main.py`
- Drop `izip` import; use built-in `zip`
- `print "..."` → `print(...)`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`
- `map(int, ...)` → `list(map(int, ...))` (map is lazy in Py3)
- Integer division: `/` → `//`

### `experimental.py`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`

### `README.md`
- Requirement updated from Python 2 → Python 3
- Commands updated from `python` → `python3`

title: Update pandas to support Python 3.13
body:
## Problem

The build fails during pandas compilation under Python 3.13.12 with the error "standard attributes in middle of decl-specifiers" in Cython-generated C++ code. This is a known incompatibility — pandas 2.2.2 predates Python 3.13 and its generated C extensions cannot be compiled against it.

## Solution

Bumped pandas from 2.2.2 to 2.3.0 in requirements.txt. Pandas 2.3.0 is the first release with official Python 3.13 support and ships pre-built wheels for it, eliminating the source compilation step that triggered the error.

### Changes
- **Modified** `requirements.txt`
